priorities.label: Overlay timing fix — REAL ROOT CAUSE was overlay timing (not FSM). voltkin.ts sustainedEffectMs 8000->500 + cutsceneOverlay.ts removed crossfadeCharacterSprite. Pre-S30 the cinematic overlay covered the screen for cinematicMs(4000)+sustainedEffectMs(8000)=12s wall-clock, EXACTLY covering Voltkin's 8s lifetime (spawns at t=4s, despawns at t=12s). User saw only the static character sprite while the actual creature lived its entire life UNDER the opaque-black overlay.

priorities.label: Screen-shake + radial spark burst — new src/render/screenShake.ts (~115 LOC) 6-tick decay +/-2px stage offset on fire-tick + 14-spark radial burst added to drawArcFlash. Tick-deterministic pseudo-random jitter direction. Wired in main.ts CREATURE_ATTACK post-dispatch when bond severed. 10 unit tests.
priorities.check_method: 10 ScreenShake unit tests passing covering: inactive default state false; active within duration true; expired at exactly duration tick false; pre-start time-travel false; full amplitude at elapsed=0; half amplitude at elapsed=duration/2; determinism (same tick -> same offset across instances); chaotic-feel (different ticks -> different offsets); trigger replaces existing shake (no stacking); reset clears state. Sparks integrated in drawArcFlash with 14 radial rays at evenly-spaced angles + deterministic seed (creature.id ^ tick) + length [12, 26]px + alpha^2 ease-out curve (front-loaded). Wired in main.ts:625 CREATURE_ATTACK post-dispatch: if (!world.bonds.has(bondId)) screenShake.trigger(world.tick). Apply in main.ts:734 screenShake.applyToStage(app.stage, world.tick) every render frame before renderer syncs (idempotent reset to (0,0) when expired).
